titleStmt.title: Letter from Clerk of the Crown and Peace, County Down, to Robert Chalmers, 26 May 1916
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: Clerk of the Crown and Peace, County Down

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p: This is a letter from Clerk of the Crown and Peace, County Down, to the Under-Secretary, Sir Robert Chalmers (1858-1938). In the letter the County Clerk writes to Chalmers that he has handed to the police a number of documents, as requested by Chalmers.Sir Robert Chalmers was appointed Under-Secretary of Ireland in 1916, replacing Sir Matthew Nathan (1862-1939) in the aftermath of the Easter Rising. Chalmers refused compensation for civilians killed and wounded in the Easter Rising and was reportedly disdainful toward the people of Ireland.

msIdentifier.collection: Chief Secretary's Office Registered Papers, CSO RP/1916/8987

correspAction.persName: Clerk of the Crown and Peace, County Down

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
COUNTY OF DOWN CROWN AND PEACE OFFICE, DOWNPATRICK. 26th May 1916 To Under Secretary, Dublin Castle No. 8737.Rex v SAMUEL HERON. Sir , I am in receipt of your letter of the 25th
instant, directing me to hand over to Police the
Documents therein mentioned. These toâday I have transmitted to the County
Inspector. Yours faithfully,
